paper_title: PtyRAD: A High-performance and Flexible Ptychographic Reconstruction Framework with Automatic Differentiation

experiment1_ssim_convergence.description: SSIM convergence comparison (Fig 3a analog) - PtyRADc vs baseline
experiment1_ssim_convergence.paper_claim: PtyRADc (positivity+sparsity) achieves comparable or higher SSIM

experiment2_depth_regularization.description: kz vs rz filter comparison (Fig 5 analog) - wrap-around artifact
experiment2_depth_regularization.paper_claim: rz filter avoids wrap-around artifacts present with kz filter

experiment3_batch_scaling.description: Iteration time vs batch size (Fig 3b analog)
experiment3_batch_scaling.paper_claim: Smaller batch sizes lead to longer iteration times

experiment4_mode_scaling.description: Iteration time vs probe modes (Fig 3c analog)
experiment4_mode_scaling.paper_claim: Iteration time scales approximately linearly with probe modes

experiment5_slice_scaling.description: Iteration time vs object slices (Fig 3d analog)
experiment5_slice_scaling.paper_claim: Iteration time scales approximately linearly with slices

experiment6_loss_comparison.description: Gaussian vs Poisson loss function comparison

notes: Reproduction uses simulated bilayer phase object (analogous to tBL-WSe2)
Running on CPU - smaller problem size used (32x32 probe, 64x64 object, 16x16 scan)
Paper uses GPU (NVIDIA A100) with larger datasets - absolute times differ
Key qualitative findings reproduced: positivity+sparsity improves reconstruction
Forward model implements Equations 1-4 (multislice with mixed states)
Loss functions implement Equations 5-6 (Gaussian/Poisson) and Eq 8 (sparsity)
Depth regularization implements Equations 10-13 (kz and rz filters)
AD-based optimization uses PyTorch Adam optimizer as in paper
Linear scaling of iteration time with modes and slices confirmed
